izations in the city Some time ago the brckayers de-
termined that the check system wrong They insisted
cash. At first there couple of of refusal
but when the brckayers quietly quit work, all the
tractors agreed to eliminate the check business.


The Press in all it's departments including members Of
the typographical union pays in cash. But there very
few unions in the city who paid in this manner at
present .tt.


Thousands upon thousands Of dollars worth of checks
are cashed every Saturday afternoon and night by the
saloons And the holders of the checks spend anywhere
from five cents to five dollars for getting them cashed by
buying drinks One saloon Main cashes 900
worth of the Stanton Meat company checks and another
saloon cashes worth of checks On the Fourth of
July it cashed about worth of checks The saloons
longer seek this business in fact they have tried to get
out Of it, but there other way for hundreds of to
get their wage money.


employers in every branch of business will take the
trouble to pay in cash. hundreds of families throughout
the city will be benefited and will bless them for the
action There is good argument for the check system
The only is that it employers little trouble
But if they would realize that it u.s. in economic and
practical best to eliminate the method they would
quickly stop it.


The Press urges the trades council to take the matter
up Immediately and take the necessary steps to
mate the plan.
